economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted operations through travel restrictions, capital project stoppages, increased PPE costs, reduced arrears collection. GBEs include Attawapiskat Power Corporation (electricity distribution) and Attawapiskat Enterprises (catering, security, resource development). Kimesskanemenow LP (winter road construction), Creewest LP (contracting), Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth LP (investments) held as portfolio investments.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - basis is that comparative budget figures are not presented in the statements of operations and change in net financial assets, a departure from PSAS. Four entities fully consolidated: AFN Band, Education Authority, Maytawaywin Authority, Development Corporation, Health Services Board (no activity). Two GBEs on modified equity basis: Attawapiskat Power Corporation (Dec 31 year-end) and Attawapiskat Enterprises. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from truncated text - Schedule 1 not fully provided. CMHC reserve fund deficiency of $889,146. Repayable to funding agencies totals $3,469,875. Prior year had a $9,600,000 impairment loss on tangible capital assets. ISC funding calculated as Fixed + Grant + Flexible + Prior year adjustments = $35,271,547. Total government transfers include ISC, FNIHB, CMHC, Nishnawbe Aski, Province of Ontario = $54,365,338. OSR includes investment income from GBEs, rental, interest, OFLP, IBA Trust, Mushkegowuk, contracting/user fees, other revenue, repayment of funding, deferred revenue movements.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
